word_list.word: every man for himself and the devil (shall| might as well|) take the hindmost
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi mˈæn fɔːɹ hɪmsˈɛlf ænd ðə dˈɛvəl ʃˌæl mˌaɪt æz wˈɛl tˈeɪk ðə hˈaɪndmoʊst

senses.definition: used to imply that in a difficult or competitive situation, individuals prioritize their own well-being and survival above others

examples.x: When the ship started sinking, the passengers panicked and it was every man for himself and the devil take the hindmost.

word_list.word: every miller draws water to (his|their) own mill
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi mˈɪlɚ dɹˈɔːz wˈɔːɾɚ tə hɪz ɔːɹ ðɛɹ ˈoʊn mˈɪl

senses.definition: used to imply that people will act in their own self-interest and use whatever resources they have available to achieve their own goals, even if it means working against other

examples.x: The politicians were accused of using their positions to benefit themselves and their families, as every miller draws water to his own mill.

word_list.word: near is my shirt, but nearer is my skin
word_list.pronunciation: nˌɪɹ ɪz maɪ ʃˈɜːt bˌʌt nˈɪɹɚɹ ɪz maɪ skˈɪn

senses.definition: used to imply that individuals have a tendency to prioritize their own needs, safety, and comfort over other external considerations

word_list.word: self-preservation is the first law of nature
word_list.pronunciation: sˈɛlfpɹɪsɚvˈeɪʃən ɪz ðə fˈɜːst lˈɔː ʌv nˈeɪtʃɚ

senses.definition: used to imply that every living creature has a natural instinct to protect itself and ensure its own survival

examples.x: The company's employees were forced to prioritize their own job security over loyalty to their colleagues when facing layoffs, as self-preservation is the first law of nature.

senses.definition: used to imply that selfish people are often willing to act in ways that benefit themselves, even if it causes harm to others or goes beyond the acceptable limits of behavior

examples.x: Even though she was fully aware of the risks, Susan refused to take the necessary precautions to prevent the spread of a contagious disease, proving that selfishness knows no bound.

word_list.word: selfishness is not living as one wishes to live, it is asking others to live as one wishes to live
word_list.pronunciation: sˈɛlfɪʃnəs ɪz nˌɑːt lˈɪvɪŋ æz wˈʌn wˈɪʃᵻz tə lˈɪv ɪt ɪz ˈæskɪŋ ˈʌðɚz tə lˈɪv æz wˈʌn wˈɪʃᵻz tə lˈɪv

senses.definition: said to imply is not simply pursuing one's own interests, but also trying to impose one's will on others, rather than respecting their autonomy and allowing them to live as they wish
